e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The Windows x86_64 PE/COFF native recipe object floor now exposes a BML generic template/class contract while preserving byte-identical COFF object emission and swappable DLL loading through the bootstrap host.
public_endpoints: local-only: Form stdlib, Windows native DLL/bootstrap tests, and Windows host workflow; no public HTTP endpoint changed
test_flows: High grammar proof: NativeObjectEmitter<TCode,TObject> and WindowsX64RecipeEmitter validate through form-pe-coff-band to verdict 16383 on the sibling kernels; fourth-arm manifest expectation is updated to the same verdict.
DLL swap proof: TestWindowsRecipeDLLLoadsCallsAndSwaps links Form-emitted PE/COFF objects into DLLs and calls swapped recipe implementations through dylib_call.
Bootstrap host proof: TestWindowsBootstrapHostExeLoadsAndSwapsFormDLLs builds the minimal host exe and swaps Form-emitted recipe DLLs behind the same exported symbol.
Regression proof: Windows fkwu socket, HTTP, self-JIT, and form-cli native verifiers still pass under sanitized runtime PATHs without Go, clang, or LLVM.
